claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 18 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 18 and not in approved education or extension period
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Child is 18 and not in approved education or extension period
circumstances: Child is 18 and not in approved education or extension period
Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. Let me first read the official guidance to ensure I give you accurate information.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for your enquiry. I'm here to help you find out if you're eligible for Child Benefit. 

To assess your eligibility, I need to ask you some questions. Let me start with the first one:

**Do you have any children, and if so, how many children are you asking about?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I have one child. I'm interested in finding out if I can claim Child Benefit for my son Alex.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. I understand you have one child, your son Alex, and you'd like to know if you can claim Child Benefit for him.

**How old is Alex?**

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you. Since Alex is 18 years old, his eligibility depends on whether he's in approved education or training, as Child Benefit can continue for children under 20 if they're in approved education or training.

**Is Alex currently in approved education or training?** (For example, A-levels, Scottish Highers, NVQs, or similar qualifications)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, he's not in any education or training at the moment. He finished school and hasn't started anything else.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. I need to ask a couple more questions to complete the assessment.

**Do you live in the UK?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you. One more important question:

**Does Alex currently live with you?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, he lives at home with me.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for providing all that information. I now have enough details to assess your eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex.

Based on the official government guidance, I'm afraid you would **not be eligible** for Child Benefit for Alex. Here's why:

Child Benefit is available for children under 16, or for young people aged 16-19 if they remain in approved education or training. Since Alex is 18 and is not currently in approved education or training, he does not meet the eligibility criteria for Child Benefit.

If Alex were to start approved education or training in the future, you could potentially claim Child Benefit again, as long as he remains under 20 years old and stays in that approved education or training.

Let me record this outcome formally.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Is there anything else I can help you with regarding Child Benefit or any other questions you may have?

reasonings: Alex is 18 years old and not in approved education or training. According to the eligibility rules, Child Benefit is available for children under 16, or for those aged 16-19 if they stay in approved education or training. Since Alex has finished school and is not in any education or training, he does not meet the age and education requirements for Child Benefit eligibility.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant lives in the UK with their 18-year-old son Alex. While the claimant meets the residency requirement, Alex does not qualify for Child Benefit because he is over 16 and not currently in approved education or training. Child Benefit eligibility for young people aged 16-19 requires them to be in approved education or training, which Alex is not.

Since Alex has finished school and is not in any education or training, he does not meet the age and education requirements for Child Benefit eligibility.

response.overall_reasoning: The claimant lives in the UK with their 18-year-old son Alex. While the claimant meets the residency requirement, Alex does not qualify for Child Benefit because he is over 16 and not currently in approved education or training. Child Benefit eligibility for young people aged 16-19 requires them to be in approved education or training, which Alex is not.
